bonuses.leadin: A novel by Eugene Burdick and William Lederer titled in reference to this older novel follows the engineer Homer Atkins and has come to name a type of obnoxious and ignorant foreign visitor. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: A novel by Eugene Burdick and William Lederer titled in reference to this older novel follows the engineer Homer Atkins and has come to name a type of obnoxious and ignorant foreign visitor. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which the narrator is left by his mistress Phuong for a man he suspects is working for the CIA, the idealist Alden Pyle.
This novelist set The Quiet American in Vietnam. This British author’s other novels set abroad include Our Man in Havana and The Power and The Glory.

The narrator of The Quiet American, Thomas Fowler, holds this profession. William Boot is a member of this profession who travels to the fictional East African country of Ishmaelia in a novel by Evelyn Waugh.
parts_sanitized: Name this novel in which the narrator is left by his mistress Phuong for a man he suspects is working for the CIA, the idealist Alden Pyle.
This novelist set The Quiet American in Vietnam. This British author's other novels set abroad include Our Man in Havana and The Power and The Glory.

The narrator of The Quiet American, Thomas Fowler, holds this profession. William Boot is a member of this profession who travels to the fictional East African country of Ishmaelia in a novel by Evelyn Waugh.
answers: The Quiet American (The Burdick and Lederer novel is The Ugly American.)
journalist [or reporter or member of the press or a foreign correspondent; accept newspaper writer; prompt on writer] (Waugh’s novel is Scoop.)

journalist [or reporter or member of the press or a foreign correspondent; accept newspaper writer; prompt on writer] (Waugh's novel is Scoop.)

bonuses.leadin: One novella set in this location describes a visitor from “Timbuctoo” who arrives to conduct a project for the UN but stays to seduce the town librarian’s daughter. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this fictional town near Madras, whose landmarks include Ellmann Street and The Boardless Hotel. It serves as the setting of a novel in which Raman makes a signboard for Daisy.

Malgudi is the creation of R.K. Narayan, who was brought to the attention of Western readers by this English author of The Heart of the Matter and The Power and the Glory, who promoted Narayan as the “Indian Chekhov.”
In this Graham Greene novel, journalist Thomas Fowler forms an ambivalent relationship with the title character, an idealistic CIA agent named Alden Pyle, who funds terrorist actions in Vietnam.

packet.name: McGill A + Louisville + Yale B + Florida A

bonuses.leadin: One character in this novel bases his worldview on a scholar with no experience on the ground in Vietnam. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which the York Harding-loving Alden Pyle is behind car and bicycle bombings. Pyle also steals Phuong away from the narrator.
This British author wrote The Quiet American and Our Man in Havana.
York Harding and Alden Pyle use this catchphrase to refer to their vision of a traditionalist resurgence in Southeast Asia that is bound to neither Communism nor Western colonialism.
